message:
refactor(diary): Consolidate sanitization into cassExport

Problem:
- Both generateDiary() and generateDiaryFast() had duplicate
  sanitization code blocks
- Sanitization was called after cassExport() in both functions
- Extra boilerplate for compiling patterns and creating runtime config
- cassExport() already accepts config and has access to sanitization
  settings

Solution:
- cassExport() now handles sanitization internally when config is passed
- Remove duplicate sanitization blocks from generateDiary() and
  generateDiaryFast()
- Simplify both functions: just call cassExport() and get sanitized
  content back
- Renumber steps in comments (removed step 2: Sanitize)

Benefits:
- DRY: Single sanitization call path
- Fewer lines of code in diary generation
- cassExport() is now the authoritative place for session content
  extraction and cleaning
- Consistent sanitization behavior across all diary types

Note: verifySanitization() check remains in generateDiary() to warn
about potential secrets that might have slipped through.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
